subject: update nexus image references
commit_message:
update nexus image references

Change-Id: Ieb9ce34ed15077e45680fe9ceef5957a05732936
Issue-ID: OOM-904
Signed-off-by: Jorge Hernandez <jh1730@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Hope K8s was able to pull this image.

Patch Set 1:

Hi,

Seems like k8s is able to pull the image.
However, nexus restarted 7 times before stabilizing. Can it be due to the removed readiness check?
On another env, without this patch it does not restart.

Patch Set 1:

Thanks Borislav.   Some more info, this image is an "standard" one from the dockerhub registry, as the mariadb one is.   I think the configuration for this container mimics the mariadb one and comes from the same source, which in theory should be correct.

The nexus container does not have dependencies with any other container, therefore the removal of that section.   Similar to the mariadb that does not depend on any other container to be ready.

I also noticed in my lab the long times you mentioned, the container being in the "running" state, although in my observation does not seem to be specific to this container itself, but more a general issue with several policy and non-policy containers.

Patch Set 1:

btw, I think the time has to do between some interactions problems between the actual download of the image (or images) such as the new one in use, nexus, and the kubernates operations reviving containers.   I restarted (with the image already being downloaded) and comes up running relatively fast.
Change has been successfully merged by Borislav Glozman
